jokes.text: Heat wave! Heat wave! Heat wave!
jokes.setup: Two guys trying to get into a pool during a heat wave
jokes.punchline: They're chanting 'heat wave' like it's some kind of mantra or rally cry
jokes.explanation: Establishes their childlike enthusiasm and lack of self-awareness, but fairly basic character work

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Heat wave!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Heat wave!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Heat wave!

jokes.text: Charlie, chug this.
jokes.setup: They're told they can't bring beer onto the premises
jokes.punchline: Their immediate solution is to chug the beer right there
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing their problem-solving approach and disregard for social norms

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Charlie, chug this.

jokes.text: We're in the middle of a terrible heat wave... and you happen to be at capacity- for us.
jokes.setup: Pool attendant explains they're at capacity
jokes.punchline: Mac's incredulous delivery implies discrimination
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the class warfare theme while showing Mac's paranoia

dialogue.speaker: Pool Attendant
dialogue.line: We're in the middle of a terrible heat wave... and you happen to be at capacity- for us.

jokes.text: Don't you get it? We got big-timed, dude. He called us lower class.
jokes.setup: Charlie doesn't understand what happened
jokes.punchline: Mac's interpretation that they were discriminated against based on class
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central theme with Mac's typical overanalysis

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Don't you get it? We got big-timed, dude. He called us lower class.

jokes.text: Ugh! Gross, dude. I will not go to that disgusting public pool. People there are gross.
jokes.setup: They need somewhere to swim
jokes.punchline: Mac's classist attitude about the public pool while being lower class himself
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's hypocrisy and self-delusion about his own class status

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Ugh! Gross, dude. I will not go to that disgusting public pool. People there are gross.

jokes.text: 'Cause Jamie Nelson drowned in it.
jokes.setup: Mac explains why the pool looks like shit now
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact mention of a drowning as the reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy delivery - casual mention of death as pool trivia

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'Cause Jamie Nelson drowned in it.

jokes.text: Ahh!
jokes.setup: Someone is doing something physical
jokes.punchline: They get hurt or startled
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy beat, too little context to evaluate properly

dialogue.line: Ahh!

jokes.text: Man, I'm gonna have to keep tinkering with the A.C. 'cause I can't feel it at all.
jokes.setup: They're in what appears to be a run-down apartment
jokes.punchline: Mac pretending their broken/nonexistent AC is just being 'tinkered with'
jokes.explanation: Shows their poverty while Mac maintains delusions of having functioning amenities

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Man, I'm gonna have to keep tinkering with the A.C. 'cause I can't feel it at all.

jokes.text: That's why I got the gold chain.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining how to approach Frank as equals
jokes.punchline: His solution is wearing a cheap gold chain
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual representation of Mac's misunderstanding of class markers

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's why I got the gold chain.

jokes.text: Don't do airplane noises. Just give him the beer.
jokes.setup: Charlie is serving Frank a beer
jokes.punchline: Mac has to specifically instruct Charlie not to make airplane noises
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific character detail - implies Charlie would naturally make airplane noises when serving drinks

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Don't do airplane noises. Just give him the beer.

jokes.text: Because you're not even supposed to be here. Frank is the audience. Just keep your mouth shut.
jokes.setup: Charlie asks why they don't get beers
jokes.punchline: Mac treating their pitch like a formal business presentation
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's delusions about business presentations and control

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Because you're not even supposed to be here. Frank is the audience. Just keep your mouth shut.

jokes.text: Boom! Pool!
jokes.setup: Mac's elaborate presentation setup
jokes.punchline: The grand reveal is just the word 'Pool!' on a sheet
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax - elaborate buildup for the most basic possible presentation

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Boom! Pool!

jokes.text: Pass. ... Just let me finish. Pass. It's too much work. What else you got?
jokes.setup: Mac trying to pitch the pool idea
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately and repeatedly dismisses it
jokes.explanation: Frank's brutal efficiency in crushing Mac's dreams

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Pass. ... Just let me finish. Pass. It's too much work. What else you got?

jokes.text: You're born into class. It's about pedigree. It's about upbringing. It has nothing to do with your present circumstances.
jokes.setup: Discussion about class
jokes.punchline: Dennis's pompous lecture about class while living in squalor
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis delusion about his superiority despite obvious evidence to the contrary

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're born into class. It's about pedigree. It's about upbringing. It has nothing to do with your present circumstances.

jokes.text: I quit on that shit. You understand? I quit on it. I don't give a rat's ass about class. I live on the fringe. Fringe class is where I am.
jokes.setup: Dennis talking about class hierarchy
jokes.punchline: Frank's bizarre declaration of 'fringe class' as his own category
jokes.explanation: Frank creating his own class category is perfectly in character and absurd

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I quit on that shit. You understand? I quit on it. I don't give a rat's ass about class. I live on the fringe. Fringe class is where I am.

jokes.text: We could go pop a hydrant.
jokes.setup: They're discussing how to cool off
jokes.punchline: Frank's solution is illegally opening a fire hydrant
jokes.explanation: Shows Frank's criminal mindset and low-class solutions

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: We could go pop a hydrant.

jokes.text: Well, we know you. You could sponsor us. I don't know what kind of idea you have about us, but we, sir, are like you.
jokes.setup: They need sponsors for the swim club
jokes.punchline: Dennis's awkward attempt to claim equality with the pool attendant
jokes.explanation: Cringe moment as Dennis tries to claim social equality he clearly doesn't have

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, we know you. You could sponsor us. I don't know what kind of idea you have about us, but we, sir, are like you.

jokes.text: Want a little something sweet?
jokes.setup: Dennis suggests sending something sweet the attendant's way
jokes.punchline: Dee's awkward interpretation as offering some kind of sexual favor
jokes.explanation: Awkward misunderstanding of Dennis's bribery suggestion

dialogue.line: Want a little something sweet?

jokes.text: Well, good for you. You keep a couple of token ones around, do you? Parade 'em around like dancing monkeys?
jokes.setup: Pool attendant mentions African-American family
jokes.punchline: Dee's incredibly offensive accusation
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy from character being accidentally racist while trying to call out racism

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Well, good for you. You keep a couple of token ones around, do you? Parade 'em around like dancing monkeys?

jokes.text: And having class means being able to mix with people that are beneath you.
jokes.setup: Dennis claiming they have class
jokes.punchline: His definition of 'class' is being able to slum it with lower people
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Dennis's twisted understanding of class and superiority

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And having class means being able to mix with people that are beneath you.

jokes.text: Your shirt doesn't even fit, by the way. If you wanna be an elitist, get yourself a tailor.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to insult the pool attendant
jokes.punchline: Her advice about getting a tailor while she's clearly not well-dressed herself
jokes.explanation: Dee's hypocritical fashion advice while being poorly dressed herself

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Your shirt doesn't even fit, by the way. If you wanna be an elitist, get yourself a tailor.

jokes.text: Oh, Jesus Christ. This is like a goddamn zoo.
jokes.setup: Dennis and Dee arrive at the public pool
jokes.punchline: Dennis's immediate horror and comparison to a zoo
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's immediate classist reaction to the public pool environment

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, Jesus Christ. This is like a goddamn zoo.

jokes.text: That girl just jumped in with her sneakers on. These people all have sneakers on.
jokes.setup: Dennis observing the public pool
jokes.punchline: His horror at people swimming with shoes on
jokes.explanation: Sets up the class differences Dennis perceives in pool behavior

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That girl just jumped in with her sneakers on. These people all have sneakers on.

jokes.text: I bartered fringe style with that guy over there. I gave him a bite of my hot dog, he's letting me use his towel.
jokes.setup: Dee asks where Frank got a towel
jokes.punchline: Frank's bizarre barter system using food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - 'fringe style' callback with absurd bartering

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I bartered fringe style with that guy over there. I gave him a bite of my hot dog, he's letting me use his towel.

jokes.text: We could be, like, 'That's where the swim-up bar is.'
jokes.setup: Looking at a rusty bike frame in the abandoned pool
jokes.punchline: Mac's delusional vision of it as a swim-up bar location
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy showing Mac's ability to see luxury in trash

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We could be, like, 'That's where the swim-up bar is.'

jokes.text: Well, I was thinking we'd be at capacity for Dennis...
jokes.setup: Charlie mentions Dennis serving them
jokes.punchline: Mac using the swim club's 'at capacity' excuse against Dennis
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the swim club rejection, showing Mac's petty revenge fantasy

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, I was thinking we'd be at capacity for Dennis...

jokes.text: I don't wanna put a tin can in the water with a string 'cause we're not eight.
jokes.setup: Charlie's tin can phone idea
jokes.punchline: Mac's attempt at maturity while standing in a trash-filled pool
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Mac drawing arbitrary maturity lines while in an absurd situation

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't wanna put a tin can in the water with a string 'cause we're not eight.

jokes.text: There hasn't been chlorine in here since Jamie Nelson.
jokes.setup: Mac mentions the water being chlorinated
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual reference to the drowning as a timeline marker
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark callback - using the drowning as a casual timeline reference for pool maintenance

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: There hasn't been chlorine in here since Jamie Nelson.

jokes.text: I was climbing up the mattress - sort of like a ladder-type situation.
jokes.setup: Mac asks how Charlie's been getting out of the pool
jokes.punchline: Charlie reveals he was using the mattress they just threw out
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff - they've just trapped themselves by removing their only escape route

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I was climbing up the mattress - sort of like a ladder-type situation.

jokes.text: Aw, shit.
jokes.setup: Realizing they just threw out their escape route
jokes.punchline: Charlie's understated reaction to their predicament
jokes.explanation: Good understatement for their serious predicament

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Aw, shit.

jokes.text: I just stepped on broken glass. Ow! Oh, my God. There are shards of broken glass all over the bottom of the pool.
jokes.setup: They're at the public pool
jokes.punchline: Dennis discovering the pool is dangerous
jokes.explanation: Basic escalation of their public pool problems

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I just stepped on broken glass. Ow! Oh, my God. There are shards of broken glass all over the bottom of the pool.

jokes.text: Well, now I know why people are wearing shoes.
jokes.setup: Dennis steps on glass
jokes.punchline: He realizes the practical reason for the shoe-wearing he was judging
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his earlier judgment, with practical revelation

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, now I know why people are wearing shoes.

jokes.text: You grease the watermelon up, you throw it in the pool. Everybody goes crazy trying to grab it. They can't grab it. Greased watermelon!
jokes.setup: Dee asks what Frank is doing with a watermelon
jokes.punchline: Frank's enthusiastic explanation of the greased watermelon game
jokes.explanation: Frank's enthusiasm for low-brow pool games fits his character perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You grease the watermelon up, you throw it in the pool. Everybody goes crazy trying to grab it. They can't grab it. Greased watermelon!

jokes.text: Which one of you little shits stole my shoes? Goddamn it!
jokes.setup: Dennis has been having problems at the public pool
jokes.punchline: His shoes have been stolen by children
jokes.explanation: Continues Dennis's downward spiral at the public pool

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Which one of you little shits stole my shoes? Goddamn it!

jokes.text: I'm putting out a feeler call. Stride, stride, stride, stride, execute.
jokes.setup: Mac is trying to escape the pool
jokes.punchline: His overly technical approach to a simple jump
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac moment - overcomplicating a simple physical task with technical jargon

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm putting out a feeler call. Stride, stride, stride, stride, execute.

jokes.text: Charlie, I do back flips every single day of my life.
jokes.setup: Charlie challenges Mac's back flip claims
jokes.punchline: Mac's ridiculous assertion about daily back flips
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac bravado about abilities he clearly doesn't have

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Charlie, I do back flips every single day of my life.

jokes.text: Well, I need to stay focused and conserve energy, and that's, you know- Come on.
jokes.setup: Charlie wants to see a back flip
jokes.punchline: Mac's weak excuse for not demonstrating his 'daily' back flips
jokes.explanation: Mac's transparent excuse-making when challenged to prove his abilities

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, I need to stay focused and conserve energy, and that's, you know- Come on.

jokes.text: What if I need to do a back flip later?
jokes.setup: Charlie wants him to do a back flip now
jokes.punchline: Mac's concern about needing to save his back flip for later
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Mac logic - rationing his fictional back flip ability

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What if I need to do a back flip later?

jokes.text: You've had a cell phone this entire time, dude? Are you kidding me?
jokes.setup: Charlie's phone rings while they're stuck
jokes.punchline: Mac's outrage at Charlie having a solution the whole time
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that they could have called for help immediately

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You've had a cell phone this entire time, dude? Are you kidding me?

jokes.text: Because getting stuck in an abandoned pool is extremely low class. Dennis will sink his teeth into it.
jokes.setup: Charlie wants to tell Dennis they're stuck
jokes.punchline: Mac's concern about appearing low class while literally stuck in trash
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Mac worried about class perception while in the most undignified situation

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Because getting stuck in an abandoned pool is extremely low class. Dennis will sink his teeth into it.

jokes.text: We're stuck in a pool!
jokes.setup: Mac tells Charlie not to reveal they're stuck
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately blurts it out
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie inability to keep a secret, perfectly timed

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We're stuck in a pool!

jokes.text: Is that Mexican music?
jokes.setup: Dennis hearing music from Mac's location
jokes.punchline: His confused question about hearing Mexican music
jokes.explanation: Sets up Mac's need to lie about their location

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Is that Mexican music?

jokes.text: It's one of the attendants. He's walking around with... a ghetto blaster... serving us drinks and just making us comfortable, giving us hats.
jokes.setup: Mac needs to explain the Mexican music
jokes.punchline: His elaborate lie about fancy pool service including 'ghetto blaster'
jokes.explanation: Mac's panicked improvisation creating an absurd luxury pool fantasy

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's one of the attendants. He's walking around with... a ghetto blaster... serving us drinks and just making us comfortable, giving us hats.

jokes.text: The attendant, you know, he just walked off with my shoes... and - it made me mad because he didn't ask. But it's great because in the end I'm gonna end up with polished shoes.
jokes.setup: Mac needs to explain Dennis yelling about stolen shoes
jokes.punchline: Mac's elaborate lie turning shoe theft into luxury shoe service
jokes.explanation: Brilliant improvisation - Mac spinning Dennis's actual misery into luxury service

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: The attendant, you know, he just walked off with my shoes... and - it made me mad because he didn't ask. But it's great because in the end I'm gonna end up with polished shoes.

jokes.text: I buy 'em all the time. You prepay for it, and then you buy it and you throw it away. There's a name for it.
jokes.setup: Mac asks about Charlie having a cell phone
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact explanation of burner phones
jokes.explanation: Shows Charlie's casual criminality and his roundabout way of explaining things

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I buy 'em all the time. You prepay for it, and then you buy it and you throw it away. There's a name for it.

jokes.text: This is like felon class.
jokes.setup: Mac realizes Charlie has a burner phone
jokes.punchline: Mac creating a new class category below their already low status
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Frank's class system with Mac's horror at sinking even lower

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: This is like felon class.

jokes.text: Yeah, we probably shouldn't get Chinese though... because those delivery guys are always Chinese... and he won't understand a word we're saying... And plus, he's probably gonna be too short.
jokes.setup: Mac suggests Chinese food
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casually racist practical concerns
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy from Charlie's casual racism, but feels somewhat cheap

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, we probably shouldn't get Chinese though... because those delivery guys are always Chinese... and he won't understand a word we're saying... And plus, he's probably gonna be too short.

jokes.text: It'll be some big Italian lug, and he'll fish us out of here.
jokes.setup: Charlie suggests pizza instead
jokes.punchline: His ethnic stereotyping about pizza delivery guys
jokes.explanation: More casual stereotyping from Charlie, shows his worldview but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It'll be some big Italian lug, and he'll fish us out of here.

jokes.text: Hey, you guys order a pizza? You gotta be kidding me. He's Chinese.
jokes.setup: Charlie expects a 'big Italian lug'
jokes.punchline: The pizza delivery guy is Chinese, contradicting Charlie's stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Charlie's expectations and stereotypes

dialogue.speaker: Pizza Delivery Guy
dialogue.line: Hey, you guys order a pizza?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You gotta be kidding me. He's Chinese.

jokes.text: I cut them off 'cause they were overshooting the bottom of my shorts.
jokes.setup: Charlie has no money in his pockets
jokes.punchline: He cut off his pockets for fashion reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - destroying functionality for imagined aesthetic improvement

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I cut them off 'cause they were overshooting the bottom of my shorts.

jokes.text: Hey, this chain's fake as shit, man.
jokes.setup: Mac offers his gold chain as payment
jokes.punchline: The delivery guy immediately identifies it as fake
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Mac's class symbol being exposed as fake

dialogue.speaker: Pizza Delivery Guy
dialogue.line: Hey, this chain's fake as shit, man.

jokes.text: Don't you understand? This pool is our bootstraps... and it's lifting us up into the middle class, dude.
jokes.setup: Charlie wants to call Dennis for help
jokes.punchline: Mac's delusional metaphor about the trash pool being their path to success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor mixing American Dream bootstrap ideology with their pathetic situation

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Don't you understand? This pool is our bootstraps... and it's lifting us up into the middle class, dude.

jokes.text: Well, just accept the fact that you're white trash, all right? Take a look at yourself. You can't do back flips. You don't know karate. You're white trash!
jokes.setup: Mac refuses to accept reality
jokes.punchline: Charlie's brutal honesty about Mac's abilities and status
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Mac's earlier claims with Charlie's rare moment of clarity

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, just accept the fact that you're white trash, all right? Take a look at yourself. You can't do back flips. You don't know karate. You're white trash!

jokes.text: Do not call the shorts white trash. Dennis told me about these shorts. They're not white trash.
jokes.setup: Charlie calls Mac white trash
jokes.punchline: Mac's specific defense of his shorts based on Dennis's authority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac moment - defending his class status through Dennis's fashion approval

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Do not call the shorts white trash. Dennis told me about these shorts. They're not white trash.

jokes.text: I put the phone in my front pocket.
jokes.setup: Charlie tries to call Dennis
jokes.punchline: The phone is in the pocket he cut off
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff - Charlie's earlier pocket decision comes back to trap them

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I put the phone in my front pocket.

jokes.text: This Filipino lady went crazy on me. She started scratching me like a velociraptor.
jokes.setup: Frank explains why he was kicked out of the other pool
jokes.punchline: His comparison of the woman to a velociraptor
jokes.explanation: Good Frank analogy mixing casual racism with dinosaur references

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: This Filipino lady went crazy on me. She started scratching me like a velociraptor.

jokes.text: We're not at capacity, 'cause I can see the pool from here. We are at capacity, sir!
jokes.setup: They're arguing about pool capacity
jokes.punchline: Dennis's literal interpretation vs the attendant's insistence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the capacity conflict with Dennis's desperate logic

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We're not at capacity, 'cause I can see the pool from here.

dialogue.speaker: Pool Attendant
dialogue.line: We are at capacity, sir!

jokes.text: I'm jumping in the pool! Yeah. Plus I'm gonna piss in it.
jokes.setup: Final confrontation at the swim club
jokes.punchline: Their threat to jump in and urinate in the pool
jokes.explanation: Final desperate threat showing how low Dennis and Dee have sunk

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm jumping in the pool!

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah. Plus I'm gonna piss in it.

jokes.text: Lamb of God who takes away - What is that? There's a lamb?
jokes.setup: Mac is doing a prayer for Jamie Nelson
jokes.punchline: Charlie's confusion about the lamb in the prayer
jokes.explanation: Charlie's literal interpretation of religious imagery

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Lamb of God who takes away

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What is that? There's a lamb?

jokes.text: Alligator tooth and snakeskin - Don't undermine my prayer with voodoo.
jokes.setup: Mac is doing Christian prayer
jokes.punchline: Charlie adds voodoo elements and Mac's horror
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Mac's Christian prayer and Charlie's voodoo additions

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Alligator tooth and snakeskin -

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Don't undermine my prayer with voodoo.

jokes.text: Admit that you are low class, just like us.
jokes.setup: Mac realizes they all failed to get into pools
jokes.punchline: Mac demanding Dennis admit their shared class status
jokes.explanation: Mac's moment of vindication about class equality

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Admit that you are low class, just like us.

jokes.text: I hope you trash bags die at the bottom of this pool like Jamie Nelson did.
jokes.setup: The class argument escalates
jokes.punchline: Dennis's cruel reference to the drowning victim
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing Dennis at his most cruel with the drowning callback

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I hope you trash bags die at the bottom of this pool like Jamie Nelson did.

jokes.text: I think I have a way for us to beat the heat tonight... Philly style.
jokes.setup: Everyone has failed at their pool attempts
jokes.punchline: Frank's solution involving opening fire hydrants
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Frank's earlier suggestion, showing it was the solution all along

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I think I have a way for us to beat the heat tonight... Philly style.
